title: Emmanuelle Poupart
content:
Managing Partner, Québec Region
People.ContactEmail epoupart@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Montréal
Known for her strategic advocacy and practical approach, Emmanuelle delivers effective solutions for clients facing complex litigation and regulatory challenges.
Emmanuelle Poupart is Managing Partner for the Québec Region and a partner in our Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group in Montréal. She specializes in civil litigation, with a particular focus on medical liability, class actions and insurance.
With a deep understanding of the healthcare and technology sectors and a talent for distilling difficult ideas into accessible concepts, Emmanuelle excels at guiding clients through high-stakes disputes and intricate regulatory matters.
As the Québec lead of the firm’s medical liability group, Emmanuelle is a recognized thought leader in medical defence and civil procedure. She advises clients in the healthcare, pharmaceutical and technology industries. She regularly represents physicians on behalf of the Canadian Medical Protective Association (CMPA) and defends companies in class actions involving medical issues, product liability and consumer protection. Her experience spans trial and appellate courts, administrative tribunals and quasi-judicial bodies.
Representative Experience:
- Successfully defended a healthcare company in Canada’s first pharmaceutical product liability class action trial, resulting in dismissal of claims related to alleged psychiatric reactions to an antibiotic.
- Pleaded before the Supreme Court of Canada to secure access to critical evidence in a class action arising from a plane crash.
- Acted for the defence in a major class action concerning recovery of accessory fees charged by Québec physicians.
- Represents clients in class actions involving, video game addiction, PFAS contamination and alleged health risks from cell phone waves.
- Advised a telecommunications company in a class action regarding advertising practices.
Emmanuelle is a Fellow of the American College of Trial Lawyers and a Fellow of Litigation Counsel of America. She sits on the Board of the Montréal Children’s Hospital Foundation, serves as President of the Board of Danse Danse, sits on the Decision Committee of the Fonds d’assurance responsabilité professionnelle du Barreau du Québec, and is a member of the Committee on Civil Procedure of the Barreau du Québec.
Emmanuelle obtained her BCL in 1994 and a graduate diploma in bioethics in 1999 from the Université de Montréal.
